food_waste.management_method: Place all cooked or uncooked food scraps, peels, and leftovers into the Green Dustbin. 🍎 Do not put plastic bags in this bin.

tissues.management_method: Used tissues and paper towels are compostable. Place them in the Green Dustbin.

paper_straw.management_method: Paper straws can be composted. Place them in the Green Dustbin after use.

clear_plastic_bottle.management_method: Empty, rinse, and crush the bottle to save space. Place it in the Blue Dustbin. These are highly recyclable.

other_plastic_bottle.management_method: For items like shampoo or detergent bottles, rinse them out and place them in the Blue Dustbin.

plastic_bottle_cap.management_method: It's best to screw the cap back onto the empty bottle before recycling. This ensures it doesn't get lost in the sorting process.

disposable_plastic_cup.management_method: Empty any liquid and give it a quick rinse. Place the clean, dry cup in the Blue Dustbin.

plastic_lid.management_method: Rinse off any food residue and place it in the Blue Dustbin. Common on coffee cups and takeaway containers.

plastic_film.management_method: Generally not recyclable in household collections. Bunch it up and place it in the Blue Dustbin as non-recyclable dry waste.

six_pack_rings.management_method: ⚠️ Cut every ring to prevent wildlife from getting entangled. Place the cut rings in the Blue Dustbin.

single_use_carrier_bag.management_method: Reuse if possible. If disposing, place it in the Blue Dustbin. Note that these can jam sorting machinery.

polypropylene_bag.management_method: These durable woven bags (like for rice or grains) can be placed in the Blue Dustbin.

crisp_packet.management_method: These are multi-layer plastics. Clean and dry them before placing in the Blue Dustbin. Recycling depends on local facilities.

spread_tub.management_method: Scrape out any remaining food and rinse the tub. Place the clean tub and its lid in the Blue Dustbin.

tupperware.management_method: The best option is to reuse. If it's broken and cannot be repurposed, place it in the Blue Dustbin.

disposable_food_container.management_method: Rinse well to remove all food residue and place the clean container in the Blue Dustbin.

other_plastic_container.management_method: If it's a hard or semi-hard plastic container, rinse it and place it in the Blue Dustbin.

plastic_utensils.management_method: Generally not recycled due to their small size. Clean them and place them in the Blue Dustbin as non-recyclable dry waste.

plastic_straw.management_method: Too small to be sorted for recycling. Dispose of it in the Blue Dustbin.

cardboard_box.management_method: Flatten the box completely. Remove any plastic tape and place it in the Blue Dustbin.

toilet_tube.management_method: Flatten the tube and place it in the Blue Dustbin. It's excellent for recycling.

egg_carton.management_method: Paper and cardboard egg cartons are recyclable and compostable. Place them in the Blue Dustbin.

drink_carton.management_method: Empty, rinse, and flatten the carton. Place it in the Blue Dustbin for recycling at specialized facilities.

meal_carton.management_method: Remove all food waste. If the carton is coated in plastic, place it in the Blue bin. If it's heavily soiled with grease, it cannot be recycled.

pizza_box.management_method: Tear off and discard any greasy sections into the Green Dustbin. Recycle the clean cardboard parts in the Blue Dustbin.

paper_cup.management_method: Empty any liquid. The plastic lining prevents composting, but many facilities can now recycle them. Place in the Blue Dustbin.

magazine_paper.management_method: Glossy paper is recyclable. Place it in the Blue Dustbin.

wrapping_paper.management_method: Cannot be recycled if it's metallic, has glitter, or is plastified. If it's plain paper, scrunch it into a ball; if it stays scrunched, it's recyclable. Place in Blue Dustbin.

normal_paper.management_method: Includes office paper, notebooks, and letters. Keep it clean and dry and place it in the Blue Dustbin.

paper_bag.management_method: Remove any non-paper handles and place the clean bag in the Blue Dustbin.

glass_bottle.management_method: Rinse the bottle and place it in the Blue Dustbin. Glass is infinitely recyclable.

glass_jar.management_method: Rinse the jar, remove the lid (recycle separately), and place the jar in the Blue Dustbin.

glass_cup.management_method: Drinking glasses are often treated and cannot be recycled with bottles/jars. Wrap carefully and place in the Blue bin as non-recyclable waste.

aluminium_foil.management_method: Clean off food residue. Scrunch the foil into a ball (at least 2 inches wide) and place it in the Blue Dustbin.

metal_bottle_cap.management_method: Collect them in a steel can or bunch them together before placing them in the Blue Dustbin so they don't get lost during sorting.

food_can.management_method: Rinse the can to remove food and place it in the Blue Dustbin.

aerosol.management_method: Ensure the can is completely empty. Do not crush or puncture it. Place it in the Blue Dustbin.

drink_can.management_method: Empty the can and place it in the Blue Dustbin. Aluminium is highly valuable for recycling.

metal_lid.management_method: Remove from jar, rinse, and place in the Blue Dustbin.

pop_tab.management_method: It is best to leave the tab attached to the aluminum can to ensure it gets recycled.

scrap_metal.management_method: Small household items like old keys, nuts, and bolts can be placed in the Blue Dustbin. For larger items, contact a local scrap dealer.

carded_blister_pack.management_method: These have a plastic bubble on a cardboard back. Separate the plastic from the cardboard. Both parts can be placed in the Blue Dustbin.

squeezable_tube.management_method: These are made of mixed materials and are hard to clean, making them non-recyclable. Place them in the Blue Dustbin.

plastified_paper_bag.management_method: Bags with a glossy, plastic coating are mixed materials and cannot be recycled. Dispose of them in the Blue Dustbin.

rope_and_strings.management_method: These can tangle in sorting machinery, so they are not recyclable. Place them in the Blue Dustbin.

shoe.management_method: Donate if they are still wearable. If not, place them in the Blue Dustbin as non-recyclable dry waste.

unlabeled_litter.management_method: If you cannot identify the material and it appears to be dry, place it in the Blue Dustbin. When in doubt, treat it as non-recyclable.

foam_cup.management_method: Styrofoam/Thermocol is not recyclable in household collections. Place it in the Blue Dustbin.

foam_food_container.management_method: Rinse off food waste. Styrofoam is not recyclable, so place it in the Blue Dustbin as non-recyclable dry waste.

styrofoam_piece.management_method: Place all styrofoam/thermocol packaging pieces in the Blue Dustbin.

garbage_bag.management_method: The garbage bag itself is considered contaminated and not recyclable. Place it in the Blue Dustbin.

battery.management_method: ⚠️ Do not throw in any household bin. Batteries leak toxic chemicals. Take them to a designated e-waste or hazardous waste collection point.

aluminium_blister_pack.management_method: Blister packs for medicine are mixed materials (plastic and metal) and are contaminated. Place them in the Red Dustbin.

broken_glass.management_method: ⚠️ For safety, wrap broken glass securely in several layers of newspaper or a cardboard box, label it 'Broken Glass,' and place it in the Red Dustbin.

plastic_gloves.management_method: Used disposable gloves are considered sanitary waste. Place them in the Red Dustbin.

cigarette.management_method: ⚠️ Make sure the cigarette is fully extinguished. Cigarette butts contain toxic plastic filters and should be disposed of in the Red Dustbin.
